pattern: Storing check-in events by passenger id and accumulating per-route totals lets all three operations run in O(1) amortised time.
hints: A check-out can only be recorded after a check-in, so the check-in station and time need to be retrievable by passenger id at check-out time.
For each (startStation, endStation) pair you only need the running total travel time and the number of completed trips — not every individual duration.
Store check-ins in one hash map keyed by passenger id; store route totals in a second hash map keyed by the station pair. getAverageTime is then a single lookup and a division.

edge_inputs.description: two passengers on same route, then query average
same passenger makes two trips on the same route; average must reflect both
single trip, average equals that trip's duration
interspersed check-ins and check-outs for distinct passengers
passenger re-uses the same id after their first trip completes
three trips on the same route accumulate correctly

stress.description: long randomised sequence of check-ins and check-outs with occasional average queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['UndergroundSystem']
    args = [[]]
    checked_in = {}
    stations = ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E']
    t = 1
    for _ in range(size):
        if checked_in and rng.random() < 0.4:
            uid = rng.choice(list(checked_in.keys()))
            station = rng.choice(stations)
            t += rng.randint(1, 20)
            ops.append('checkOut')
            args.append([uid, station, t])
            del checked_in[uid]
        elif checked_in and rng.random() < 0.3:
            uid = rng.randint(1, 1000)
            if uid not in checked_in:
                station = rng.choice(stations)
                ops.append('checkIn')
                args.append([uid, station, t])
                checked_in[uid] = station
                t += rng.randint(1, 5)
        else:
            uid = rng.randint(1, 1000)
            if uid not in checked_in:
                station = rng.choice(stations)
                ops.append('checkIn')
                args.append([uid, station, t])
                checked_in[uid] = station
                t += rng.randint(1, 5)
    for uid in list(checked_in.keys()):
        t += rng.randint(1, 20)
        ops.append('checkOut')
        args.append([uid, rng.choice(stations), t])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: keys drawn from a large id space; all checked-in passengers are checked out at the end

complexity.space: O(P + R) where P = active passengers, R = distinct routes
